channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: New enhancements for merchant initiated transactions with the Google Pay API
item.description: Google has enhanced its Google Pay API to improve merchant-initiated transactions by allowing clearer definitions for future payment terms. This update aims to increase transparency and reduce transaction declines, benefiting both merchants and users.

item.title: Subagents have arrived in Gemini CLI
item.description: Gemini CLI has launched subagents, which are specialized agents designed to manage complex tasks while keeping the main session efficient. These agents can be customized and run in parallel, improving productivity and preventing context issues.

item.title: Speeding Up AI: Bringing Google Colossus to PyTorch via GCSFS and Rapid Bucket
item.description: Google Cloud has unveiled a new integration that connects Rapid Storage to PyTorch, significantly enhancing AI training speeds by reducing latency and increasing throughput. This allows developers to improve training times without changing their code.

item.title: Databricks can't seem to shake authors' copyright claim that could result in 'extraordinary' damages
item.description: Databricks faces a class action lawsuit from authors claiming its LLM was trained on copyrighted material, which could lead to significant damages. The court is demanding more information from Databricks regarding the claims.

item.title: Fedora 44 is out – countless versions of it
item.description: Fedora Linux 44 has been released with new bootable container images and storage options, catering to various CPU families. This version enhances user experience with multiple formats available.

item.title: Sanctioned Chinese AI Firm SenseTime Releases Image Model Built for Speed
item.description: Chinese AI firm SenseTime has launched a new image model optimized for speed, focusing on open-source solutions due to US tech restrictions. The model is designed to run on domestically produced chips.

item.title: HP vs. Dell: I've tested dozens of laptops from both brands, and here's my advice
item.description: A comparison of Dell and HP laptops reveals that while both brands offer quality products, they cater to different user needs and preferences. The analysis breaks down the key differences between their models.

item.title: Cloudflare says autocrats, wars and elections caged the internet in Q1
item.description: Cloudflare reported significant internet disruptions in Q1 2026, attributing them to government actions, wars, and other incidents. This highlights ongoing challenges in global internet stability.

item.title: Yet another experiment proves it's too damn simple to poison large language models
item.description: A recent experiment demonstrated the ease of poisoning large language models, where a security engineer misled AI bots about a fictional card game championship. This raises concerns about the reliability of AI-generated information.

item.title: Our readers can't stop buying these 10 gadgets - and No. 4 really surprised us
item.description: ZDNET's readers have shown strong interest in a list of popular gadgets, with one item surprisingly capturing attention. The article highlights consumer trends in tech products.

item.title: AI evals are becoming the new compute bottleneck
item.description: AI evaluations are increasingly becoming a bottleneck in computing, impacting efficiency and performance in AI development. This shift highlights the growing complexity of AI systems and their evaluation processes.

item.title: Google Photos uses AI to make the iconic closet from ‘Clueless’ a reality
item.description: Google has utilized AI to recreate Cher's iconic closet from the movie 'Clueless', showcasing the potential of AI in fashion and personalization. This innovation reflects how AI can enhance user experiences in creative ways.

item.title: More Gemini features are coming to Google TV
item.description: Google TV has introduced new Gemini features, including tools like Nano Banana and Veo, to enhance photo and video transformations. This update aims to improve user engagement and content creation.

item.title: NASA boss: Make Pluto A Planet Again
item.description: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman announced plans to contest Pluto's status as a planet despite budget constraints. This move could reignite public interest in planetary science and the debate over Pluto's classification.

item.title: Is Facebook adding Gen Z phrases to your shared posts? You're not alone, bestie
item.description: Facebook users have reported unexpected slang appearing in their posts, likely due to AI algorithms. This trend highlights how AI is influencing social media communication and user interactions.

item.title: Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 7 vs. Motorola Razr Ultra (2026): I compared both, and it's not even close
item.description: A comparison between the Samsung Galaxy Z Flip 7 and Motorola Razr Ultra reveals that the Flip 7 excels in durability and AI features, while the Razr Ultra focuses on hardware. This analysis helps consumers make informed choices in the foldable phone market.

item.title: The best VPN for small businesses in 2026: Expert tested and reviewed
item.description: An expert review of VPNs for small businesses highlights top options like Surfshark and Proton VPN, focusing on security and usability. This information is crucial for businesses seeking to protect their data online.

item.title: After years of Linux, I tried GhostBSD and found it incredibly stable - and nearly unbreakable
item.description: A user shares their positive experience with GhostBSD, a FreeBSD-based operating system, noting its stability and reliability compared to Linux. This suggests a growing interest in alternative operating systems among tech enthusiasts.

item.title: CISA flags data-theft bug in NSA-built OT networking tool
item.description: CISA has issued a warning about a data-theft vulnerability in GrassMarlin, an NSA-developed networking tool. This alert underscores the importance of cybersecurity measures in protecting sensitive information.

item.title: Health is Tim Cook's defining legacy - and your Apple Watch proves it
item.description: Tim Cook's introduction of the Apple Watch has significantly influenced health tracking and wellness, establishing a legacy centered on health technology. This shift reflects the growing importance of health in technology.

item.title: Granite 4.1 LLMs: How They’re Built
item.description: Granite 4.1 LLMs are discussed in detail, focusing on their construction and capabilities. This exploration highlights advancements in language model technology.

item.title: AWS plants more tombstones in the application graveyard
item.description: AWS announced its latest move into the applications business at an event in San Francisco, marking a significant shift in its strategy. This continues AWS's trend of expanding its service offerings.

item.title: All the evidence unveiled so far in Musk v. Altman
item.description: The Musk v. Altman trial is revealing early evidence from OpenAI's history, including emails and documents. This case could impact public perception and trust in AI companies.

item.title: Ubuntu’s AI plans have Linux users looking for a ‘kill switch’
item.description: Canonical's announcement of AI features in Ubuntu has sparked backlash from users seeking alternatives without these additions. This reflects broader concerns about AI integration in software.

item.title: GitHub: Woah, a genuinely helpful AI-assisted bug report that isn't total slop. Here, Wiz, take this wad of cash
item.description: Wiz researchers discovered a significant vulnerability in GitHub's infrastructure, aided by AI, leading to a substantial financial reward. This showcases the effectiveness of AI in security research.

item.title: Tumbler Ridge families are suing OpenAI
item.description: Families of victims from a school shooting in Canada are suing OpenAI for negligence, claiming the company failed to act on flagged ChatGPT activity. This case raises serious ethical questions about AI responsibility.

item.title: ChatGPT downloads are slowing — and may cause problems for OpenAI’s IPO
item.description: ChatGPT's growth is slowing, with a significant increase in uninstalls, which may hinder OpenAI's IPO prospects. This trend reflects increasing competition in the chatbot market.

item.title: Larry’s risky business
item.description: Oracle's pivot to AI is unconventional, focusing on unique strategies rather than following typical industry trends. This could signal a new direction for tech companies in AI.

item.title: Adaptive Thinking: Large Language Models Know When to Think in Latent Space
item.description: Recent developments in LLMs show they can perform intermediate reasoning during queries, enhancing their performance. Understanding this relationship is crucial for optimizing AI inference.

item.title: DeepInfra on Hugging Face Inference Providers 🔥
item.description: DeepInfra has launched on Hugging Face Inference Providers, expanding options for developers using AI models. This enhances accessibility and usability of AI technologies.

item.title: IREN Price Target Cut as Bernstein Sees Firm Dumping Bitcoin Mining for AI
item.description: Bernstein analysts predict that IREN will shift focus from Bitcoin mining to AI cloud services, marking a significant strategic pivot for the company.

item.title: Samsung’s “Glasses” Confirmed
item.description: Samsung has confirmed the development of its Galaxy Glasses, smart eyewear designed for daytime use, similar to Meta's Ray-Bans.

item.title: o11y-reflect 0.7.1
item.description: The o11y-reflect 0.7.1 update provides insights into the performance issues of AI coding agents, helping developers optimize their usage.

item.title: MSI Prestige 14 Flip AI+
item.description: The MSI Prestige 14 Flip AI+ laptop impresses with exceptional battery life, setting a new standard for Intel's Panther Lake processors.

item.title: enact-langchain 0.2.1
item.description: The enact-langchain 0.2.1 release introduces tools for the ENACT Protocol, enabling trustless escrow for AI agents on the TON blockchain.

item.title: nvidia-dali-tf-plugin-cuda130 2.1.0
item.description: NVIDIA has released a TensorFlow plugin for CUDA 13.0, enhancing performance for AI developers using TensorFlow.

item.title: Musk faces off with OpenAI in court over broken promises
item.description: Elon Musk is facing legal challenges from OpenAI over alleged broken promises, with a ruling expected by late May.

item.title: Better Hardware Could Turn Zeros into AI Heroes
item.description: Experts discuss how better hardware can enhance the performance of large AI models, despite concerns about diminishing returns.

item.title: nvidia-dali-tf-plugin-cuda120 2.1.0
item.description: NVIDIA has released a TensorFlow plugin for CUDA 12.0, providing developers with updated tools for AI applications.

item.title: How is GitHub changing Copilot pricing? #tech
item.description: GitHub is transitioning Copilot to a usage-based billing model starting June 1, changing how developers will be charged for the service.

item.title: I tried Gemini on Android Auto and it's worse than Google Assistant in every way that matters
item.description: The author found Gemini on Android Auto to be inferior to Google Assistant, highlighting its shortcomings in usability and functionality. This comparison raises concerns about the effectiveness of new AI integrations in vehicles.

item.title: Microsoft stopped protecting Windows 10 — The fix is only $10
item.description: Microsoft has ceased support for Windows 10, urging users to upgrade to Windows 11 Pro for enhanced security and performance. This move emphasizes the importance of keeping software updated for optimal protection.

item.title: stellars-claude-code-plugins 1.1.19
item.description: The release of Claude Code plugins version 1.1.19 aims to enhance autonomous development workflows. This update reflects ongoing advancements in AI tools for developers.

item.title: enact-langchain 0.2.0
item.description: The launch of LangChain tools for the ENACT Protocol (version 0.2.0) introduces trustless escrow capabilities for AI agents on the TON blockchain. This development signifies progress in secure AI transactions.

item.title: OpenAI brings its models to Amazon's cloud after ending exclusivity with Microsoft
item.description: OpenAI's generative AI models are now available on Amazon's cloud, following the end of its exclusivity with Microsoft. This shift allows broader access to OpenAI's technology for developers and businesses.

item.title: ETtech Explainer: How the Altman vs Musk trial is unfolding in court
item.description: The trial between Elon Musk and OpenAI has begun, with Musk claiming the company has deviated from its original mission. The case will explore liability and the implications of OpenAI's shift to a for-profit model.

item.title: fortigate-readonly-mcp added to PyPI
item.description: A new local read-only MCP server for Fortigate administration over SSH has been added to PyPI. This tool enhances network security management capabilities.

item.title: OpenAI Models on Amazon Bedrock
item.description: OpenAI's models are now integrated into Amazon Bedrock, allowing users to build AI agents using OpenAI's technology directly on AWS. This integration signifies a strategic partnership shift.

item.title: OpenAI's latest AI models, Codex now available on Amazon Bedrock
item.description: OpenAI's latest AI models, including Codex, are now accessible on Amazon Bedrock, following a contract change with Microsoft. This availability enhances the tools developers can use on AWS.

item.title: SAC-pytorch 0.1.0
item.description: The release of Soft Actor Critic (SAC) version 0.1.0 for Pytorch provides a new reinforcement learning tool for developers. This addition supports advancements in AI training methodologies.
